intro: This course teaches you the libraries that developers use to build webpages: React, TypeScript, and more.
To earn your Front-End Development Libraries Certification:
- Complete the five required projects to qualify for the certification exam.
- Pass the Front-End Development Libraries Certification exam.

In these lessons, you will get an introduction to JavaScript libraries and frameworks. You will learn about the roles of JavaScript libraries and frameworks, single page applications (SPAs) and the issue surrounding them, and React, the most popular front-end JavaScript library.

challengeOrder.title: What Is the Role of JS Libraries and Frameworks, and Why Are They Used in the Industry?
What Are Single Page Applications, and What Are Some Issues Surrounding Them?
What Is React, and What Is It Commonly Used For?
What Are Components in React, and How Do They Work?
How Do Default and Named Exports Work in React Components?
What is Vite, and How Can It Be Used to Set Up a New React Project?

meta.name: Introduction to JavaScript Libraries and Frameworks

intro: In the previous lessons, you learned how to work with components in React.
In this workshop, you will build a reusable Navbar component using React.
In this lab, you'll use React to build a reusable footer.
In these lessons, you will learn how to work with data in React. You will learn about props and how to pass them around, conditional rendering, how to render lists, and how to use inline styles.

challengeOrder.title: How Do You Pass Props from a Parent Component to a Child Component in React?
How Does Conditional Rendering Work in React Components?
How Do You Render Lists in React?
How Do Inline Styles Work in React?

intro: In this workshop, you will learn how to work with props by building a reusable profile card component.

meta.name: Build a Reusable Profile Card Component

intro: In this lab, you'll create a mood board using React.
You'll practice how to pass data from a parent component to a child component using props.
Review basic React concepts to prepare for the upcoming quiz.
Test your knowledge of React basics with this quiz.
In these lessons, you will learn about working with state and responding to events with React.

challengeOrder.title: How Do Events Work in React?
What is State, and How Does the useState Hook Work?

intro: In this workshop, you will continue to learn about the useState() hook by building an application that hides and shows a piece of text on the screen.
In this lab you'll build a Color Picker.
You'll practice using state and hooks to manage the properties of an element.
In these lessons, you will learn about effects and referencing values with React.

challengeOrder.title: How Do You Reference Values Using Refs?
What Are Effects in React, and How Does the useEffect Hook Work?
How Can You Create Custom Hooks in React?

meta.name: Understanding Effects and Referencing Values in React

intro: In this workshop, you will continue to learn about the useEffect() hook by building an application that fetches fruit data from an API based on user input and displays the results dynamically.
In this lab you'll build a one-time password generator.
You'll practice using the useEffect hooks to create a timer and generate a random OTP.
Before you're quizzed on React state and hooks, you should review what you've learned.
Open up this page to review working with state, custom hooks and more.
Test what you've learned about React's useState and useEffect hooks with this quiz.
In these lessons, you will learn about working with forms in React.

challengeOrder.title: How Do Forms Work in React?

intro: In this workshop, you will build a superhero application form.
In this lab, you'll build an Event RSVP form using React.
You'll practice using the useState hook to manage form input and display user responses.
In these lessons, you will learn about data fetching and memoization in React.

intro: In this workshop, you'll use the useMemo() and useCallback() hooks in React to build a simple shopping list app. You'll learn more about state and the lifecycle of React components, and how to use memoization to reduce re-renders and make your apps more efficient.
In these lessons, you will learn about routing in React, React frameworks, and dependency management tools.

intro: For this lab, you'll build a currency converter app.
You'll use React state, memoization, and controlled components to convert between currencies.
